# Rugby Timer Requirements Quality Checklist: Rugby Referee Timer

**Purpose**: Validate the clarity, completeness, consistency, and measurability of the rugby referee timer requirements before task generation and implementation.
**Created**: 2026-04-12
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are all referee-critical timer states defined, including not started, running, paused, half ended, match ended, expired, and cleared states? [Completeness, Spec Key Entities]
- [x] CHK002 Are all v1 scoring actions documented with point values and counters: try, conversion, penalty goal, and drop goal? [Completeness, Spec FR-011-FR-015]
- [x] CHK003 Are yellow-card countdown behavior and red-card persistent indicator behavior both specified without implying a red-card expiry timer? [Completeness, Spec FR-017-FR-019]
- [x] CHK004 Are variant setup requirements complete for built-in presets, one-minute half adjustments, sin-bin duration, and conversion duration overrides? [Completeness, Spec FR-001-FR-004]
- [x] CHK005 Are activity-recording requirements complete for exact rugby labeling, fallback labeling, and validation expectations? [Completeness, Spec FR-023-FR-024]
- [x] CHK006 Are storage requirements limited and explicitly tied to preferences rather than match-history persistence? [Completeness, Plan Technical Context]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK007 Is the phrase "synchronized" clarified through a single display-refresh/state-snapshot expectation rather than left as a subjective visual claim? [Clarity, Spec FR-009, Plan Summary]
- [x] CHK008 Is "most prominent" defined enough for downstream UI planning to decide main countdown placement and relative visual priority? [Ambiguity, Spec FR-007]
- [x] CHK009 Is "when screen size allows" bounded by a requirement or planning constraint for what must remain visible on smaller watches? [Ambiguity, Spec FR-008, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK010 Are haptic alert thresholds specified with exact timing and affected timer types? [Clarity, Spec FR-020]
- [x] CHK011 Is the phrase "closest supported activity-label fallback" sufficiently constrained to avoid arbitrary non-rugby labels? [Ambiguity, Spec FR-024, Research ActivityRecording]
- [x] CHK012 Are team-label editability expectations clear enough to avoid expanding setup flow beyond the lightweight v1 scope? [Ambiguity, Spec Assumptions]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK013 Are pause/resume rules consistent across user stories, edge cases, functional requirements, and the data model? [Consistency, Spec Clarifications, Spec FR-005, Data Model MatchClockState]
- [x] CHK014 Are conversion timer replacement rules consistent across clarification notes, edge cases, functional requirements, and the UI contract? [Consistency, Spec Clarifications, Spec FR-016, Contract Scoring Controls]
- [x] CHK015 Are red-card requirements consistently described as persistent indicators rather than countdown timers across all artifacts? [Consistency, Spec FR-018-FR-019, Data Model DisciplineSanction]
- [x] CHK016 Are scoring counters consistently required in the spec and data model for try, conversion, penalty goal, and drop goal? [Consistency, Spec SC-003, Data Model TeamState]
- [x] CHK017 Are Garmin compatibility requirements aligned between the spec's fenix 6+ goal and the plan's API 4.1.6 minimum decision? [Consistency, Spec FR-022, Plan Technical Context]
- [x] CHK018 Are the constitution's referee-first and lightweight constraints reflected consistently in scope exclusions and quickstart validation? [Consistency, Spec FR-027, Quickstart Fallback Policy]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK019 Can each user story's independent acceptance statement be objectively evaluated without requiring implementation-specific knowledge? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec User Scenarios]
- [x] CHK020 Are timer synchronization success criteria measurable enough to distinguish acceptable behavior from visible drift? [Measurability, Spec SC-001]
- [x] CHK021 Is the one-display-refresh scoring update criterion measurable for all scoring actions, not only tries? [Measurability, Spec SC-003]
- [x] CHK022 Is watch readability defined with enough measurable criteria beyond "readable" and "representative watch-size validation"? [Ambiguity, Spec SC-004]
- [x] CHK023 Are haptic success criteria measurable for devices that support haptics and clearly exempt devices that do not? [Measurability, Spec SC-005]
- [x] CHK024 Is regression preservation measurable with named behavior groups rather than a broad non-specific claim? [Measurability, Spec SC-006]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK025 Are primary flows covered for match setup, match clock control, scoring, sanctions, activity recording, and match end/save? [Coverage, Spec User Scenarios]
- [x] CHK026 Are alternate flows covered for custom rugby timing values and targets that cannot support exact rugby activity labeling? [Coverage, Spec User Story 4, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK027 Are exception flows defined for activity recording unavailability, unsupported haptics, and smaller watch layouts? [Gap, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK028 Are recovery flows specified for accidental pause/resume/end-half actions or incorrect score/sanction entry? [Gap]
- [x] CHK029 Are requirements clear for clearing red-card indicators and expired yellow-card sanctions? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases, Data Model DisciplineSanction]
- [x] CHK030 Are requirements defined for what happens when the final half ends and activity recording must be stopped or saved? [Gap, Contract Match Clock Controls]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK031 Are overlapping conversion timer requirements complete and unambiguous after the replacement clarification? [Edge Case, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK032 Are simultaneous 60-second haptic alerts for multiple timers addressed in requirements, including priority or coalescing expectations? [Gap, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK033 Are yellow-card timers that cross a half boundary specified clearly enough for the selected rugby variant rules? [Ambiguity, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK034 Are requirements defined for multiple concurrent yellow-card sanctions on the same or different teams? [Gap, Spec FR-017]
- [x] CHK035 Are requirements defined for how custom timing changes affect already-active timers versus future timers only? [Gap, Spec FR-003-FR-004]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK036 Are accessibility requirements complete for color-blind-friendly design, non-color cues, contrast, and watch-scale readability? [Completeness, Spec FR-019, Spec FR-021]
- [x] CHK037 Are performance requirements stated in user-observable terms and linked to the single-timebase plan decision? [Consistency, Spec SC-001, Plan Performance Goals]
- [x] CHK038 Are device compatibility requirements specific enough to decide manifest target inclusion and exclusion? [Clarity, Spec FR-022, Quickstart Fallback Policy]
- [x] CHK039 Are privacy/security assumptions documented for local-only storage and absence of network dependency? [Assumption, Spec FR-026, Plan Storage]
- [x] CHK040 Are regression isolation requirements traceable from constitution to spec and plan quality gates? [Traceability, Constitution VI, Spec FR-028, Plan Constitution Check]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK041 Are Garmin API dependency assumptions documented with the selected API level and rationale? [Dependency, Research Connect IQ API 4.1.6]
- [x] CHK042 Are Fit permission and single activity-recording session constraints captured in requirements or planning artifacts? [Dependency, Research ActivityRecording]
- [x] CHK043 Are assumptions about built-in rugby variant presets explicit enough to create variant data without further product decisions? [Assumption, Spec Assumptions]
- [x] CHK044 Are assumptions about haptic support documented consistently in spec, quickstart, and plan? [Assumption, Spec SC-005, Quickstart Manual Validation]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK045 Is there any conflict between the user's fenix 6+ compatibility goal and the plan's API 4.1.6 minimum that needs an explicit product decision? [Conflict, Spec FR-022, Plan Technical Context]
- [x] CHK046 Is "count-up or realtime timer" normalized to a single canonical term before task generation? [Ambiguity, Spec FR-008]
- [x] CHK047 Is the fallback policy for non-rugby activity labels specific enough to avoid implementation-time disagreement? [Ambiguity, Spec FR-024, Quickstart Fallback Policy]
- [x] CHK048 Are requirements explicit about whether team labels are editable in v1 or fixed to Home/Away? [Ambiguity, Spec Assumptions]

## Resolution Summary

- Completed during the 2026-04-12 requirements revision pass.
- Scope decisions added: fixed Home/Away labels for v1, canonical count-up active match timer, coalesced simultaneous haptics, lightweight correction actions, deliberate confirmation for end-half/end-match/save, multiple yellow-card handling, active-timer override behavior, and rugby-equivalent activity fallback/exclusion policy.
- Supporting artifacts updated: `spec.md`, `plan.md`, `research.md`, `data-model.md`, `contracts/ui-interaction-contract.md`, `quickstart.md`, and `tasks.md`.

## Notes

- This checklist validates the written requirements and planning artifacts, not the implementation.
- Items marked incomplete should be resolved before `/speckit.tasks` if they could change task scope or acceptance criteria.
